subject: English
q:
Choose appropriate adjectives for the noun in the following sentence.

They have a ___, ___ and ___ class representative.
feedback:
An adjective is a part of speech that tells us more about a noun. It describes the quality or state of the noun, or we can say that it modifies the noun. More than one adjective can also be used to describe the same noun. Here, the adjectives smart, funny and friendly can be used to describe the noun class representative. The other adjectives don't make sense according to the context of a class representative. They describe the physical attributes of a shape or object, rather than a person. A class representative is a student who speaks to teachers or school management on behalf of the other students.

They have a smart, funny and friendly class representative.

q: Which one of the following sentences contains more than one adjective for the same noun?

subject: English
q: Which of the following is NOT true about adjectives?
options: Adjectives are used to describe nouns.
More than one adjective can be used to describe the same noun.
Adjectives are used to describe pronouns.
Adjectives are used to describe other adjectives.
feedback: The only false statement is that adjectives are used to describe other adjectives. Adjectives do not modify or describe each other. Adjectives are words that give additional information about nouns or pronouns. They describe the qualities or states of being of the nouns, or we can say that they modify the nouns. You can use more than one adjective in a sentence to describe the same noun.

subject: English
q: Adjectives are words that ___.
options: specify whether the nouns are general or specific
feedback:
Adjectives are words that give additional information about nouns. They describe the qualities or states of nouns, or we can say that they modify the nouns. Look at the following sentence:

Your house is beautiful.

In this case, the word beautiful is an adjective because it gives us additional information about the house, a noun.

Words that replace nouns in a sentence are called pronouns. Words that specify whether the nouns are general or specific are called articles and words that give additional information about verbs are called adverbs.

subject: English
q: Adjectives generally answer one of three questions about the nouns or pronouns they describe. They are:
options: what part?, how fast?, can it?
why not?, what time?, how large?
which one?, what kind?, how many?
when to?, why was?, did you?
feedback:
The correct response is which one?, what kind?, and how many?

Adjectives may be used to indicate specific things, thus answering the question: 'which one?' For example, this chair, or Humera's basket.

Additionally, adjectives may tell us about the kind or quality of a person, place, or thing, therefore, answering the question: 'of what kind?' For example, Lahore is a large city or Maarej has brown eyes.

Finally, adjectives may tell us about the quantity of a person, place, or thing, thus answering the question: 'how much?'. For example, I have little patience or Maham ate all the biscuits.

subject: English
q: Which sentence in "A Brief History of Writing" tells us what kind of things were written on clay tablets by the Mesopotamians and the Sumerians?
options: Pictographs are images of physical things, such as horses.
Tablets from this era have been found today, with writing in a script known as cuneiform.
...the Sumerians and Mesopotamians recorded their language on clay tablets baked in the sun.
Experts believe that [they] created pictographs...to keep a record of items owned, items bought, and items for sale.
feedback:
We know from the passage that clay tablets were used to record business and trade. We know that writing was in the form of pictographs. Neither of these describe the actual words and items that were written down. The only sentence that does this is:

"Experts believe that [they] created pictographs...to keep a record of items owned, items bought, and items for sale."
article:
1. There was a time when human beings lived in caves. Imagine living in a home in the side of a mountain, without doors or windows, without sofas or chairs, or beds and pillows. Humans once lived like that. Back then, these humans had only a few things to worry about. They needed to eat, they needed clothes, and they needed shelter. Yet, even with such simple lives, human beings still wanted to tell stories through very simple pictures and symbols. This was over 40,000 years ago!

2. Many, many years later, humans began to advance, and they began using more difficult ways to communicate with each other. Life was no longer lived in caves but in houses. In addition to seeking food and shelter, humans spent their time in trade and manufacturing. They lived in large communities—cities—and travelled long distances to buy and sell their goods. At this time, most of mankind lived in what was known as the Fertile Crescent.

3. The Fertile Crescent was a strip of farmland that included three major countries: ancient Egypt, Mesopotamia, and Sumer. Historians believe the civilisations of the Crescent were highly advanced. As neither paper nor papyrus had been discovered in this era, the Sumerians and Mesopotamians recorded their language on clay tablets baked in the sun. Tablets from this era have been found today, with writing in a script known as cuneiform. This is probably the first instance of writing in human history.

4. In those days, humans had not created letters or alphabets. Written language was visual, using something called pictographs. Pictographs are images of physical things, such as horses. Experts believe that the Sumerians and Mesopotamians created pictographs to record trades and business deals, to keep a record of items owned, items bought, and items for sale.

5. While cuneiform may be the first example of a visual language, the most famous is probably Egyptian hieroglyphics (pronounced “hi-ro-gli-fix”).
